description: Add marketplace.json registration to generate_scaffold_script() TypeScript output
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/workspace_init/mod.rs and locate generate_scaffold_script() at line 285
Add readFileSync to the fs import in the generated TypeScript string
After the existing plugin.json writeFileSync call, add TypeScript logic to read .ai/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json
If marketplace.json exists, parse it; if not, create default structure with name 'local-repo-plugins', owner, metadata, and empty plugins array
Check for duplicate plugin name via plugins.some(p => p.name === name)
If not duplicate, push new entry { name, source: './<name>', description: 'TODO: describe <name>' } to plugins array
Write updated marketplace.json back with JSON.stringify(data, null, 2) + newline
Wrap marketplace registration in try/catch with process.stderr.write warning on failure (warn-and-continue strategy)
Run cargo build --workspace to verify the Rust string literal compiles
Run cargo fmt --check to verify formatting

description: Add settings.json enabledPlugins autoenable to generate_scaffold_script() TypeScript output
steps: In generate_scaffold_script() in mod.rs, after the marketplace.json registration block, add TypeScript logic to read .claude/settings.json
If settings.json exists, parse it; if not, create .claude/ directory and start with empty object
If enabledPlugins key is missing from settings object, create it as empty object
Construct plugin key as `${name}@local-repo-plugins`
Check if pluginKey already exists in enabledPlugins; if not, set it to true
Write updated settings.json back with JSON.stringify(settings, null, 2) + newline
Wrap settings registration in try/catch with process.stderr.write warning on failure (warn-and-continue strategy)
Update the success message at end of script to include registration status
Run cargo build --workspace to verify compilation
Run cargo fmt --check to verify formatting

description: Handle edge cases in scaffold-plugin.ts: missing files, missing keys, duplicates, parse errors
steps: Verify marketplace.json creation from scratch when file does not exist (mkdirSync .claude-plugin with recursive: true)
Verify settings.json creation from scratch when file does not exist (mkdirSync .claude with recursive: true)
Verify enabledPlugins key is created when missing from existing settings.json
Verify duplicate plugin name in marketplace.json plugins array is skipped (not appended twice)
Verify duplicate pluginKey in enabledPlugins is skipped (not overwritten)
Verify JSON parse errors in marketplace.json emit warning to stderr and skip registration without exiting
Verify JSON parse errors in settings.json emit warning to stderr and skip autoenable without exiting
Verify plugin directory files are always created even when registration steps fail
Run cargo build --workspace and cargo test --workspace to verify

description: Add insta snapshot test for generate_scaffold_script() full TypeScript output
steps: Add insta as a dev-dependency in crates/libaipm/Cargo.toml (latest stable version)
Verify insta works with workspace lint configuration (derive macros emit internal #[allow] which is permitted)
In crates/libaipm/src/workspace_init/mod.rs tests section, add scaffold_script_snapshot test
Call generate_scaffold_script() and pass result to insta::assert_snapshot!(content)
Run cargo test --workspace once to generate the initial .snap file
Review the snapshot file at crates/libaipm/src/workspace_init/snapshots/ and accept it with cargo insta review
Verify the snapshot contains all expected TypeScript: readFileSync, marketplace.json logic, settings.json logic, enabledPlugins, local-repo-plugins
Run cargo test --workspace to confirm snapshot test passes
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings to verify no lint issues

description: Update existing unit test scaffold_script_is_nonempty with new content marker assertions
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/workspace_init/mod.rs and locate scaffold_script_is_nonempty test at line 644
Add assertion: content.contains("readFileSync")
Add assertion: content.contains("marketplace.json")
Add assertion: content.contains("settings.json")
Add assertion: content.contains("enabledPlugins")
Add assertion: content.contains("local-repo-plugins")
Run cargo test --workspace -p libaipm to verify updated test passes

description: Add new unit test scaffold_script_registers_in_marketplace for marketplace.json TypeScript logic
steps: In mod.rs tests section, add scaffold_script_registers_in_marketplace test
Call generate_scaffold_script() and store result
Assert content contains marketplace.json path construction (join with .claude-plugin and marketplace.json)
Assert content contains .some( for duplicate detection
Assert content contains .push( for array append
Assert content contains source: `./${name}` pattern
Assert content contains local-repo-plugins as marketplace name
Run cargo test --workspace -p libaipm to verify

description: Add new unit test scaffold_script_enables_in_settings for settings.json TypeScript logic
steps: In mod.rs tests section, add scaffold_script_enables_in_settings test
Call generate_scaffold_script() and store result
Assert content contains settings.json path construction
Assert content contains @local-repo-plugins key format pattern
Assert content contains enabledPlugins object creation/check
Assert content contains writeFileSync for settings.json
Run cargo test --workspace -p libaipm to verify

description: Add unit test to cross-check marketplace name between generate_marketplace_json() and generate_scaffold_script()
steps: In mod.rs tests section, add scaffold_script_marketplace_name_matches_generator test
Call generate_marketplace_json(false) and parse it as serde_json::Value
Extract the top-level name field (should be 'local-repo-plugins')
Call generate_scaffold_script() and assert it contains the same marketplace name string
This prevents drift between the Rust-generated marketplace.json and the TypeScript hardcoded name
Run cargo test --workspace -p libaipm to verify

description: Add e2e test: scaffold script registers new plugin in marketplace.json
steps: In crates/aipm/tests/init_e2e.rs, add has_node() helper that checks node --version availability
Add scaffold_script_registers_in_marketplace_json e2e test
Gate the test with if !has_node() { return; } at the start
Run aipm init --marketplace in a temp directory
Execute node --experimental-strip-types .ai/starter-aipm-plugin/scripts/scaffold-plugin.ts my-new-plugin via std::process::Command
Assert the command succeeds (exit code 0)
Read .ai/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json and parse as serde_json::Value
Assert plugins array has 2 entries (starter + my-new-plugin)
Assert second entry has name 'my-new-plugin' and source './my-new-plugin'
Run cargo test --workspace to verify

description: Add e2e test: scaffold script enables new plugin in settings.json
steps: In init_e2e.rs, add scaffold_script_enables_in_settings_json test
Gate with has_node() check
Run aipm init --marketplace in temp dir, then run scaffold script with my-new-plugin
Read .claude/settings.json and parse as serde_json::Value
Assert enabledPlugins['my-new-plugin@local-repo-plugins'] == true
Assert enabledPlugins['starter-aipm-plugin@local-repo-plugins'] == true (existing entry preserved)
Run cargo test --workspace to verify

description: Add e2e test: scaffold script creates plugin directory structure
steps: In init_e2e.rs, add scaffold_script_creates_plugin_directory test
Gate with has_node() check
Run aipm init --marketplace then scaffold my-new-plugin
Assert .ai/my-new-plugin/aipm.toml exists
Assert .ai/my-new-plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json exists
Assert .ai/my-new-plugin/skills/my-new-plugin/SKILL.md exists
Run cargo test --workspace to verify

description: Add e2e test: scaffold multiple plugins with no duplicates in marketplace.json
steps: In init_e2e.rs, add scaffold_script_multiple_plugins_no_duplicates test
Gate with has_node() check
Run aipm init, then scaffold plugin-a, then scaffold plugin-b
Read marketplace.json and assert plugins array has exactly 3 entries (starter + a + b)
Read settings.json and assert enabledPlugins has exactly 3 keys
Run cargo test --workspace to verify

description: Add e2e test: scaffold script rejects existing plugin directory
steps: In init_e2e.rs, add scaffold_script_rejects_existing_plugin test
Gate with has_node() check
Run aipm init, scaffold my-plugin, then scaffold my-plugin again
Assert second run exits with non-zero code
Assert stderr contains 'already exists'
Run cargo test --workspace to verify

description: Implement missing BDD steps: directory/file existence (data tables, no-directory, no-file, directory-exists)
steps: Open crates/libaipm/tests/bdd.rs
Add #[then] step for 'the following directories exist in {string}:' with cucumber data table — iterate rows, assert each path is_dir()
Add #[then] step for 'there is no directory {string} in {string}' — assert !path.exists() || !path.is_dir()
Add #[then] step for 'there is no file {string} in {string}' — assert !path.exists()
Add #[given] step for 'a directory {string} exists' — std::fs::create_dir_all
Run cargo test --workspace to verify BDD scenarios using these steps pass

description: Implement missing BDD steps: starter plugin manifest assertions
steps: In bdd.rs, add #[then] step for 'the starter plugin manifest contains the package name {string}' — read .ai/starter-aipm-plugin/aipm.toml, assert contains name = value
Add #[then] step for 'the starter plugin manifest contains a version of {string}' — same file, version = value
Add #[then] step for 'the starter plugin manifest contains the plugin type {string}' — same file, type = value
Add #[then] step for 'the starter plugin manifest is valid according to aipm schema' — read file and call libaipm::manifest::parse_and_validate()
Add #[then] step for 'the starter skill contains {string} in the frontmatter' — read skills/scaffold-plugin/SKILL.md, assert contains string
Run cargo test --workspace to verify BDD scenarios pass

description: Implement missing BDD steps: marketplace.json assertions
steps: In bdd.rs, add #[then] step for 'the marketplace.json name is {string}' — read .ai/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json, parse JSON, assert v['name'] == expected
Add #[then] step for 'the marketplace.json contains a plugin named {string}' — parse JSON, search plugins array for matching name
Add #[then] step for 'the marketplace.json plugin {string} has source {string}' — parse JSON, find plugin by name, assert source matches
Add #[then] step for 'the marketplace.json plugins array is empty' — parse JSON, assert plugins array length is 0
Run cargo test --workspace to verify marketplace.json BDD scenarios pass

description: Implement missing BDD steps: Claude settings.json assertions
steps: In bdd.rs, add #[then] step for 'the Claude settings contain {string}' — read .claude/settings.json, assert raw string contains value
Add #[then] step for 'the Claude settings marketplace path is {string}' — parse JSON, assert extraKnownMarketplaces.local-repo-plugins.source.path == expected
Add #[then] step for 'the Claude settings contain {string} at the top level' — parse JSON, assert top-level key exists
Add #[then] step for 'the Claude settings enable {string}' — parse JSON, assert enabledPlugins[key] == true
Add #[then] step for 'the Claude settings file preserves the custom content' — parse JSON, assert custom keys present
Add #[given] step for 'a file {string} with custom content exists in {string}' — write JSON file with known keys
Run cargo test --workspace to verify Claude settings BDD scenarios pass

description: Implement missing BDD steps: workspace manifest and gitignore assertions
steps: In bdd.rs, add #[then] step for 'the manifest contains a {string} section' — read aipm.toml, assert contains section header string
Add #[then] step for 'the manifest contains members {string}' — read aipm.toml, assert contains members = ["value"]
Add #[then] step for 'the manifest contains plugins_dir {string}' — read aipm.toml, assert contains plugins_dir = "value"
Add #[then] step for 'the gitignore contains {string}' — read .ai/.gitignore, assert contains string
Run cargo test --workspace to verify workspace-init BDD scenarios pass

description: Final validation: cargo build, test, clippy, and fmt all pass with zero warnings
steps: Run cargo build --workspace and verify zero errors
Run cargo test --workspace and verify all tests pass
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings and verify zero warnings
Run cargo fmt --check and verify no formatting issues
Review any skipped BDD scenarios and confirm they are expected (Node.js gating)
Verify no #[allow(...)] or #[expect(...)] attributes were added (per CLAUDE.md lint policy)
